pages.name: Beyond the Investigation — Rel Astra Index

text.content:
Sandbox layer for Rel Astra. The party is in the city for Phase 4 (and possibly Vector D in Phase 5). Investigation pressure is high but the city itself is large enough — 50,000 souls, four named districts, multiple temples, the broad Aerdy commercial economy — that downtime is genuinely possible. The anchors here are deliberately old-world: most have been at their posts for thirty or forty years, and they have seen worse seasons than this one.

What's in this journal

  • Per-PC anchors — Rel Astra contacts and locations for each PC.
  • Civic calendar — Rel Astra's Aerdy-imperial rhythm; the harbor commission's procedural days; the four temple festivals visible from the Old City precincts.
  • Carousing & information-gathering — d10 tables for an evening at a Goldsmiths' Quarter inn, a morning at the Old City market, an afternoon at the Brass Sextant or a Garden Quarter bath-house.
  • Crafting, training, recuperation — Rel Astra's deeper offerings, including Aerdy magical-theory training that Loftwick and Hardby cannot match.
  • Sub-team mini-quests — five short evening scenes.

How Rel Astra differs from Loftwick and Hardby

  • Rel Astra is old. Buildings are older, families are older, professional reputations are older. The city does not respect youth or speed; it respects continuity. PCs who treat Rel Astra like Loftwick — energetic, direct, helpful — will be mistaken for petitioners or for trouble. The right manner is unhurried.
  • The Aerdy imperial frame is everywhere. Even the Old Harbor Ward, walled off from the imperial gaze, runs on Aerdy time and uses Aerdy formality. PCs accustomed to Yeomanry or Hardby plainness will find the formality cold; it is not coldness, it is age.
  • The conspiracy is at home here. Phase 4's Pressure Clock has only four segments but each segment is heavier. The sandbox-layer NPCs are deliberately uninvolved with the conspiracy and will remain so; their value is exactly that they are rooms where the party can rest from the conspiracy without leaving the city.

Cam — the halfling rogue

Anchor location: Rinya's, a small fence-and-pawn operation tucked into the Old Harbor Ward's eastern edge, two streets from the Goldsmiths' Quarter wall. A polished walnut shopfront with a brass scale-and-anchor in the window.

Anchor NPC: Rinya Dane, an Aerdy half-elf fence in her late forties (looks early thirties; half-elven longevity), inherited the shop from her human mother eleven years ago. Slim, sharp-faced, dresses in dark Aerdy walking-coats. Polite without being warm; respects competence and pays for it. Hesp Olfair (Cam's Hardby anchor) has corresponded with her since both were teenagers; her note opens Rinya's door.

What she offers: 50-60% fence-rate buy-and-sell. Information on Rel Astra's Aerdy-imperial customs procedures and which routes around them are still open. The names of two private courier-operators in the Goldsmiths' Quarter who handle small high-value packages. A small cup of strong coffee Rinya brews herself.

What she needs (if Cam offers): A piece passed through her shop six months ago — a small Aerdy gold-and-jet brooch — has reappeared in the Goldsmiths' Quarter's open market in a way that suggests it was stolen back from her customer. Rinya would like the chain traced; she is not sure whether she has been robbed-of-a-resold-piece or whether her customer is selling and re-stealing his own jewelry to her. (Mini-quest 1.)

Elle — the halfling monk

Anchor location: The Temple of Heironeous's training yard, in the Old City. (See the Old City district map.) A sand-floored courtyard adjacent to the temple proper, with practice posts, a small armory of training weapons, and a covered colonnade for rainy days.

Anchor NPC: Brother Ostrik Vaerin, a militant brother of Heironeous (human, Aerdy, mid-fifties, tall for an Aerdy, broad shoulders, missing the small finger of his left hand from a southern campaign). Welcomes Elle's halfling discipline as a counterpoint to his own size; spars with her in the morning before his clergy duties. Speaks plainly and is one of the few Rel Astra NPCs who does not bother with Aerdy formality.

What he offers: Sparring (any morning at first light, before the temple's noon service). Use of the training yard at any hour. Access to the temple's small library of pre-Aerdy and Aerdy martial-philosophy texts (Elle's Loftwick chapter does not have these). A small bowl of barley-porridge in the temple's refectory.

What he needs (if Elle offers): A young Aerdy noble has been fencing at the Heironeous yard three mornings a week without proper introduction or temple sponsorship. Brother Ostrik suspects the noble is preparing to challenge someone privately and would like Elle to read the noble's bearing — the temple's own clergy cannot, by the noble's-rights protocols, approach the matter directly. (Mini-quest 2.)

Kitty — the chthonic tiefling druid

Anchor location: Three-Tree Grove, a small olive-and-cypress grove a mile north of Rel Astra's Customs Quay along the coastal road, set in a fold of the cliffs above a small private spring.

Anchor NPC: Edril Thence, an old Aerdy druid (human, late seventies, tall, white-bearded, dresses in a faded oxblood-and-cream linen robe of an order Kitty has not encountered before — the order is local to coastal Aerdy and is small). Does not advertise his practice; the Three-Tree Grove is known to a handful of Aerdy locals as a quiet place to walk. Welcomes Kitty's chthonic ancestry without comment; Edril has met older things in his career and considers Kitty's lineage merely interesting.

What he offers: Quiet at the grove. Coastal-druidic practice Kitty has not seen in Loftwick or the salt-fens — Aerdy-coastal druidry is structurally different from the Old Faith and shares some forms with Suel coastal practices. Conversation about the small things that move along the Aerdy coast at night; Edril notices things and remembers them. Three nights of safe lodging in a small tent he keeps for visitors.

What he needs (if Kitty offers): A small population of Aerdy coastal otters has begun avoiding the spring at the grove's center for the first time in Edril's memory. He has not been able to find the cause and does not believe it is a natural shift. He would value Kitty's reading. (Mini-quest 3.)

Gianni — the human ranger

Anchor location: The Aerdy imperial garrison's training annex, attached to the Customs Quay's imperial barracks. A sand-floored shooting yard, a covered armory, and a small officers' coffee-room overlooking the harbor.

Anchor NPC: Lieutenant Korven Mada, an Aerdy imperial archer (human, late thirties, dark-skinned, lean), seconded to the customs garrison from the imperial-coastal regiment two years ago. Welcomes Gianni's professional credentials (Yeomanry militia archery is not Aerdy imperial archery, but the disciplines have been corresponding for centuries); shares techniques and competes in friendly matches.

What he offers: Use of the imperial training yard (Gianni's standing requires a written sponsor's note from Halask or Astor, easily obtained). Aerdy imperial archery technique — distinct from Yeomanry militia archery and worth a season of study. The chance to read the garrison's coastal-road incident log (does not include conspiracy material; includes routine bandit activity, customs disputes, and weather notes).

What he needs (if Gianni offers): A coastal-road incident log entry from three weeks ago notes an unusual track — "not horse, not man, not common beast" — at the road's bend two miles north of the city. Lieutenant Mada has not had time to investigate; the garrison is short of patrols. He would like Gianni's eye on the tracks before he commits a militia detail. (Mini-quest 4.)

Selvara — the human sorcerer

Anchor location: The Aerdy College of Commercial Law's quiet annex, in the Goldsmiths' Quarter — a small reading room and study attached to the College's main building, used by junior magisters and their guests. Contains a corner specifically for arcane theory the College does not formally teach but tolerates.

Anchor NPC: Magister Ilen Vaden, a junior magister of the College (human, Aerdy, mid-forties, lean, holds an arcane chair the College keeps quiet about — the College's Aerdy commercial-court orientation does not formally include sorcery, but Vaden's chair was endowed eighty years ago by a sympathetic donor and has never been formally closed). Recognizes Selvara as a sorcerer on first meeting; introduces himself by his full title and his quiet specialty.

What he offers: Use of the quiet annex (Selvara's standing requires Halask's introduction or Master Ferrick's letter; both work). Access to the College's restricted-arcane reading list — three folio volumes of pre-Aerdy magical theory not available in Loftwick or Hardby. Tea, and the chance to meet two of Magister Vaden's correspondents (one a Suel theorist, one a retired Aerdy court magician) when they are in town.

What he needs (if Selvara offers): A small folio of pre-Suel sorcery has come into the College's possession via an estate auction; Magister Vaden does not have time to catalogue it before the College's annual review. He would like Selvara, during her visit, to read three folios and produce a brief written summary — a courtesy to the College and a chance for Selvara to encounter material she would not otherwise see. (Mini-quest 5.)

Alicia — the warlock

Anchor location: Mistress Veth-Avir's archive, a small private archive of sealed correspondence and pact-related documents in the Old City, three streets from the Temple of Pholtus.

Anchor NPC: Mistress Veth-Avir, an old Aerdy archivist (human, late seventies, white-haired, dresses in dark linen), specializes in sealed correspondence between practitioners of various pact-based traditions. Recognizes Alicia's pact-marks on first meeting; her archive includes (among other things) one of the few existing copies of an old Aerdy commentary on Pact-of-the-Blade traditions specifically.

What she offers: Use of the archive's reading room (Alicia's standing requires Trina Alvere's introduction; Trina has corresponded with Veth-Avir for decades). Three folio volumes specifically on Pact-of-the-Blade theory. The archive's quiet — the room is windowless and Veth-Avir has, by some old courtesy, never lit a candle a Pact-of-the-Blade visitor did not light first.

What she needs (if Alicia offers): A small portion of the archive's pact-bond correspondence has, over the last decade, been disturbed in a way Veth-Avir cannot explain. The seals are intact; the order is wrong. She would like Alicia, during her visit, to sit in the archive for one quiet morning and listen for whatever Pact-of-the-Blade-trained ear might hear. (Optional mini-quest, GM discretion. The disturbance is not connected to the Phase 4 conspiracy; it is a separate small mystery the archive has carried for years.)

pages.name: Civic Calendar — What's Happening in Rel Astra This Week

text.content:
The Aerdy imperial year

Greyhawk's twelve-month calendar applies, but Rel Astra observes the four festivals with Aerdy imperial flavor. Local emphasis differs from Yeomanry's:

  • Needfest — Rel Astra's quietest civic week. The Old Harbor Ward closes its commercial business for two days; the Customs Quay continues at half-pace; the Old City's three temples (Heironeous, Pholtus, Nerull) hold their largest public observances. The Naelax embassy holds a private new-year reception (Galenix attends if his house arrest permits).
  • Growfest — Aerdy planting festival; modest local observance because Rel Astra is a port. The Goldsmiths' Quarter holds a small public reading of new commercial-court precedent (Halask attends most years).
  • Richfest — Aerdy summer festival; major local observance. Three days of harbor games (similar to Hardby's, with imperial sponsorship). The harbor commission holds a public meeting on the festival's third day.
  • Brewfest — minor in Rel Astra. The Aerdy imperial frame does not emphasize the harvest the way Yeomanry does. The Old City's three temples observe quietly.

The Rel Astra week

DayWhat's openNotes
StardayGoldsmiths' Quarter at full activity; Old Harbor Ward markets (medium); Customs Quay full; Old City quietAerdy commercial day. Counting-houses at peak. Halask is at the Brass Sextant.
SundayThree Old City temples (full); other commerce at minimumReligious observance day. Heironeous noon service is the largest; Pholtus afternoon service smaller; Nerull dawn service quietest.
MoondayHarbor commission committee day; counting-houses full; Old Harbor Ward markets (full)Harbor commission's committee meetings; not public-facing.
GodsdayOld Harbor Ward Council in session; Goldsmiths' Quarter full; Customs Quay fullCouncil session day. Astor (Belven) attends; petitioners present in the morning.
WaterdayCounting-houses full; commerce slower; College of Commercial Law in sessionMagister Vaden (Selvara's anchor) holds his weekly office hours.
EarthdayCounting-houses full; harbor commission public petition hour; commerce fullHarbor commission's public petition hour, second to fourth bell. Open to citizens and accredited visitors.
FreedayMarkets (medium); counting-houses closed; commerce at half-pace; entertainments fullThe Goldsmiths' Hall holds a public lecture series third Freeday of each month. Aerdy commercial-court advocates attend by tradition.

The Aerdy temple calendar

Each of the three Old City temples has its own observance schedule. Useful for setting scenes:

  • Temple of Heironeous — noon service Sunday (public), morning training open to invited adherents (every day except Sunday). Annual major observance: The Vow of Valor, on the first day of Reaping. Brother Ostrik attends all.
  • Temple of Pholtus — afternoon service Sunday (public; the formal service emphasizes light and law in Aerdy imperial framing). Annual major observance: The Cleaving of Shadow, on the longest day of summer (Richfest's first day).
  • Temple of Nerull — dawn service Sunday (smaller; quiet, attended primarily by the bereaved and the elderly). Annual major observance: The Long Night, on the longest night of winter (Needfest's heart). Aerdy convention: do not enter the Temple of Nerull on the days immediately preceding or following a personal death; the bereaved take precedence.

Local events the party may run into

  • The Brass Sextant morning (any day between the seventh and ninth bells; Halask's daily anchor). Public.
  • The Goldsmiths' Hall public lecture (third Freeday of each month, central east-west street of the Goldsmiths' Quarter). Open to the public.
  • The harbor commission public petition hour (Earthday, second to fourth bell, the Harbor Commission Hall). Open to citizens and accredited visitors with cause.
  • Magister Vaden's College office hours (Waterday, second through fifth bell, the Aerdy College of Commercial Law). By appointment for non-students; Halask's letter or Master Ferrick's letter is sufficient.
  • The Three-Tree Grove morning (any day at first light; Edril Thence is most easily found at this hour). Quiet.

An evening at a Goldsmiths' Quarter inn

Cost: 6-10 sp passable; 1-2 gp comfortable; 6-12 gp memorable (Rel Astra's pricing is the highest in the campaign). Persuasion or Insight DC 14 (Rel Astra is the hardest to read of the four cities — the formality is real, not performance). On success, roll d10.

d10What you heard
1An Aerdy commercial-court advocate is openly speculating that Halask's Sereth case will become a textbook procedural reference within five years.
2A junior partner at one of the Goldsmiths' Quarter counting-houses has been seen having dinner three nights a week with a man whose accent is Greyhawk-Foreign-Quarter. Nobody thinks it is significant. (It is not, in this phase.)
3The Naelax embassy has hosted three private dinners in the last fortnight. Galenix's house arrest does not extend to social hosting on his own grounds. The dinners' guest lists have been short and discreet.
4The harbor commission's vacant seats are being discussed at the inn next door — but in Aerdy commercial circles, not Yeomanry-aligned ones, and the discussion is professional rather than political.
5A scholar from the College of Commercial Law has just published a small monograph on pre-Aerdy paleography. The monograph mentions, in passing, "hands trained outside the standard schools"; nobody at the inn has yet read it carefully. (Magister Vaden has the book.)
6A retired Aerdy ship-captain at the next table is wearing a small silver pin in the shape of a tower-and-three-waves. He is sixty-eight; the pin was his mother's. He does not know what the design means.
7The Brass Sextant has been short of its preferred Aerdy-coastal coffee for two weeks. Halask drinks the substitute without comment.
8The harbor commission's clerk has been working late three nights a week. His name is on the lease for an apartment two streets from the Hall of the Compass that nobody currently lives in. (Routine; the clerk is sub-letting to a cousin.)
9A Pholtus brother is openly arguing with a Heironeous brother at the inn's other table about the proper procedural response to a particular kind of moral question. The argument is dignified and going nowhere.
10An Aerdy poet (in town from Hardby; possibly the same poet from the Hardby salon table) is reading a new piece in the inn's back room. The piece is good. The poet is collecting names of patrons; he asks the speaker if Trina Alvere of the Yeomanry is by any chance acquainted. (Salons connect.)

A morning at the Old City market

Cost: free, plus 3-8 sp for purchases (Rel Astra prices). Investigation or Persuasion DC 13. On success, roll d10.

d10What you saw or heard
1A small folio of Suel poetry is being sold by an old Aerdy bookseller for far below its value. He does not know what he has. (Selvara may notice; Magister Vaden would pay 8 gp gladly.)
2The fishmongers' row has the year's best smoked Aerdy-coastal silverfish. 1 gp for an evening's eating.
3A Heironeous lay-acolyte has set up a small public-court at the corner of the market for the morning, hearing minor petitions. The acolyte is twenty-two and earnest. (Brother Ostrik will be told later in the day; he tolerates these informal courts but will note who attended.)
4The Naelax embassy's housekeeper is at the market early, buying for what is clearly a substantial dinner. She does not say for whom.
5A small flower-seller has the year's first cypress-cones. (Old Faith ingredient; Edril Thence at the Three-Tree Grove will note them. Kitty may purchase a few.)
6The Aerdy imperial herald reads a routine garrison bulletin at the third bell. The bulletin includes a small note about coastal-road patrol scheduling. (Routine.)
7A Halfling traveler is selling a small bag of Aerdy-stamped silver well below its value. He is honest; he is unfamiliar with Aerdy markup conventions. Cam may help him price correctly without taking a cut. (Optional small kindness scene.)
8An Aerdy commercial-court advocate hurries past with a sealed dispatch case. (Halask's office in Phase 4: occasionally dispatches advocates run errands. Not a hook.)
9A small sea-otter pup is sitting on a fish-stall, having stolen a herring. The fishmonger is laughing. (No one has named this pup; if Kitty does, the GM may decide the pup remembers.)
10A Pholtus lay-cleric is selling small candles "for the cleaving of shadow." (Pholtus calendar reference; in Phase 4 the festival is approaching. Selvara may notice the symbology.)

An afternoon at the Brass Sextant or a Garden Quarter bath-house

The Brass Sextant is Halask's daily anchor — listed in his NPC entry. For non-investigation downtime, the party may use it for unhurried Aerdy-coastal conversation; Persuasion DC 13. On success, roll d10. Garden Quarter bath-houses are public and excellent for atmosphere; Persuasion DC 12 to make the afternoon yield.

d10What you learned at the Brass Sextant or in a bath-house
1An old retired Aerdy customs official remembers, with some prompting, a tower-and-three-waves countermark that came through Aerdy customs forty years ago. He cannot recall the case; the memory is just a fragment.
2The Goldsmiths' Hall lecture series next month will feature a Suel-trained scholar on the topic of pre-imperial sealing materials. (In Phase 4: foreshadowing the "E." thread; in Phase 5: now actionable.)
3A Naelax-embassy junior houseman is at the bath-house, off-duty. He is talkative when relaxed. (Not an intelligence source for the conspiracy; he genuinely does not know what is going on inside the embassy beyond his housework.)
4The Aerdy commercial-court is considering closing the Magister Ostrach bench for renovation in the spring. The renovation will take three months; trials in progress will continue. (Halask is unconcerned.)
5A Pholtus brother and a Pholtus sister are arguing in the bath-house's dressing-room about whether the Cleaving of Shadow ritual must be conducted at noon or at the longest day's actual zenith (which differs slightly). They will continue arguing for years.
6An Aerdy poet is in town and has been writing at the Brass Sextant most mornings. (Possibly the same poet who connects to Hardby's salon table.)
7A small fragment of Aerdy commercial-court gossip: a junior advocate named Tellis Maro has been disagreeing with Halask in court on procedural points more often than is professionally warranted. Halask is patient about it.
8The Three-Tree Grove is mentioned by a retired Aerdy customs official as a place where his grandmother used to walk. He does not know it is still there.
9The bath-house's masseur recognizes Selvara's pre-light signature and makes a small respectful sign of the College's restricted-arcane chair. He is the only one in the room who notices.
10Halask, late in his morning, mentions to the speaker (whoever they are; Halask treats the morning as a public office hour) that the case is going as he expected and the procedural papers are arriving as anticipated. He does not elaborate. (Halask's calm is itself information.)

Crafting and gear

ServiceProviderCost / TimeNotes
Mundane weapon repair / fittingGoldsmiths' Quarter smiths (multiple)1-2 gp / 1 dayRel Astra pricing is highest of the four cities; quality is correspondingly excellent.
Custom weapon commissionMaster Drael, the Old Harbor Ward weaponerfull PHB price + 50% / 7-14 daysThe campaign's best weaponsmith. Aerdy specialty: the rapier and the duelling sword. Alicia's slim gold rapier could be improved or replaced here.
Armor repair / commissionGoldsmiths' Quarter armorers2-4 gp / 3-7 daysHalfling fitting available without surcharge at most shops; Aerdy convention.
Aerdy-imperial pattern bowLieutenant Mada introduces (the imperial bowyer)40-80 gp / 1-2 weeksAvailable to Gianni only by Mada's introduction.
Spell-component refresh (any grade)Magister Vaden at the College quiet annex2-5 gp + components at cost / 1-3 daysVaden's access to rare components exceeds Hardby's; rare-of-rare components may take 2-3 weeks.
Druidic focus / coastal-druidic preparationsEdril Thence at the Three-Tree Grovefree for Kitty / 1-2 daysEdril does not deal in coin. A small offering is appropriate.
Identifying a magic item (common to rare)Magister Vaden5-30 gp / 1-3 daysVaden identifies any item Master Ferrick or Mistress Korr cannot. Very rare or unique items may require longer.
Pact-related item analysisMistress Veth-Avir at her archive (Alicia only)by donation / 1 dayVeth-Avir's specialty.
Aerdy commercial-court paleographyHalask (Phase 4 only; investigation NPC)variesInvestigation resource; not a downtime activity.

Training

Rel Astra is the campaign's best training city for Aerdy-imperial languages, formal philosophy, and arcane theory.

Skill / ToolTrainerNotes
Old Aerdi (language)Magister Vaden, Mistress Veth-Avir, Brother Ostrik, multiple othersRel Astra has the deepest pool of Old Aerdi teachers in the campaign.
Suloise (language)Magister VadenVaden's specialty.
Aerdy imperial cipher (writing)Magister Vaden, by special arrangementDifficult to obtain; Vaden teaches it sparingly.
Coastal-druidic (Druidic dialect)Edril Thence (only for druids)Edril will not teach a non-practitioner. The dialect differs from Old Faith Druidic.
Pact-of-the-Blade theoryMistress Veth-Avir (Alicia only)The campaign's deepest training for Alicia's specific tradition.
Aerdy imperial archeryLieutenant Korven Mada (Gianni only)Standard rate; competitive matches at the imperial yard half-rate.
Investigation (skill)Halask (Phase 4 only; investigation NPC)Not a downtime resource.
History (Aerdy commercial-law focus)Magister VadenStandard rate; Vaden's library is the best.
Athletics / Acrobatics (Heironeous-style)Brother OstrikStandard rate; Ostrik's training emphasizes martial discipline.

Recuperation

ServiceProviderCost / DayNotes
Goldsmiths' Quarter inn (high-end)multiple5-10 gp / dayExcellent recuperation. Three days fully restores. The campaign's most expensive inns.
Old Harbor Ward inn (modest)multiple2-4 gp / dayStandard recuperation; cheaper than Goldsmiths' Quarter.
The Heironeous training yard's pilgrim cellsBrother Ostrik (Elle free; others by his invitation, 2 gp donation)2 gp / dayBonus: same as the Open Way (recuperator may end two persistent effects rather than one).
The Three-Tree Grove (camping)Edril Thence (Kitty free; others by Edril's invitation)freeBonus: same as the Reedrush — recuperator wakes with one Inspiration die after the third day. Edril may also offer a brief coastal-druidic blessing on departure.
The College of Commercial Law's quiet annex (back room)Magister Vaden (Selvara free; others by Halask's introduction, 4 gp/day)4 gp / dayQuiet, scholarly. Includes use of the restricted reading list.
The Garden Quarter bath-housesmultiple, public1-3 gp / dayHalf-day baths and quiet recovery. Atmospheric. Standard recuperation.
Cure disease / restoration (clerical)Temple of Heironeous (martial wounds, exhaustion); Temple of Pholtus (curse-related); Temple of Nerull (necrotic / undead-related)50-200 gp / 1 dayEach temple specializes. Higher-grade illnesses may require Rauxes or beyond.

1. Cam — "The Brooch That Returned" (rogue affinity)

Rinya Dane sold an Aerdy gold-and-jet brooch six months ago to a Goldsmiths' Quarter junior partner. The brooch has reappeared in the Goldsmiths' Quarter open market, being sold by a different man. Rinya would like the chain traced.

The chain: the original buyer wore the brooch twice and then lost it (he believes) at a Goldsmiths' Hall public lecture. A pickpocket lifted it; a fence three streets away bought it; the fence's apprentice walked it across to the open market without his master's knowledge; the apprentice is the man Cam will see selling it. Nothing about the chain is conspiracy-related; it is the small ordinary motion of stolen jewelry through a city's underbelly.

How it resolves: Cam observes at the open market. Investigation DC 13 to identify the apprentice's movements. The right answer is to recover the brooch from the apprentice (Persuasion DC 12 or Sleight of Hand DC 13), trace the chain back to the lecture-pickpocketing event, and report to Rinya. Rinya will pay 5 gp for the recovery and request the apprentice not be reported (the apprentice's master is a fence Rinya wants to keep open as a competitor; an arrest disrupts the broader trade).

Reward: Rinya raises Cam's fence-rate to 60% standard, 65% on referral. She introduces Cam to the two private courier-operators in the Goldsmiths' Quarter she had previously only mentioned by name.

2. Elle — "The Young Noble's Steel" (monk affinity)

Brother Ostrik has noticed a young Aerdy noble fencing at the Heironeous yard three mornings a week without proper temple sponsorship. The noble — call him Lord Pellan Veth-Aurus, twenty-three, recently returned from a Rauxes posting — is preparing for a private duel. Brother Ostrik suspects the duel is over a small Aerdy-noble matter (a slight at a dinner, a poem misattributed) and would like to confirm before deciding whether to formally close the yard to Lord Pellan.

How it resolves: Elle observes Lord Pellan's morning practice. Insight DC 13 to read his bearing — he is preparing for a real duel, not posturing; the duel is in five days; the opponent is unknown to Elle. Elle may approach Lord Pellan in the yard's small courtyard at the end of practice; he will be courteous to a halfling visitor in monk's discipline, which is the rarity that lets Elle in. Persuasion DC 14 to learn the opponent's name (a Lord Aerin Mada, no relation to Lieutenant Mada — common Aerdy surname). The right answer is to report Lord Pellan's preparation to Brother Ostrik privately, advise that the temple yard's neutrality be quietly extended (close it to neither lord, but post a discreet temple observer at the duel itself if it cannot be prevented). The wrong answer is to confront Lord Pellan; he will not abandon the duel and will be offended.

Reward: Brother Ostrik grants Elle a small Heironeous lay-token (no in-game power; functions as a recognition-token at any Aerdy Heironeous temple). The token is the size of a halfling's thumbprint and can be carried unobtrusively.

3. Kitty — "The Otters That Stopped Drinking" (druid affinity)

Edril Thence has noticed that the Aerdy coastal otters near the Three-Tree Grove have stopped drinking from the small private spring at the grove's center for the first time in his memory. The cause is not natural; Edril cannot find it.

The cause: a small Aerdy bronze coin, dropped into the spring three weeks ago by a passing pilgrim who was distracted, has corroded slightly and produced a faint metallic taste in the water. The otters can taste it; Edril cannot. The coin is unremarkable except that it is the wrong shape for the spring.

How it resolves: Kitty walks to the grove and sits at the spring (Animal Handling DC 11 to read the otters' movements correctly). Survival or Investigation DC 12 to find the coin (it is at the spring's bottom, half-covered by silt). The right answer is to retrieve the coin (with appropriate respect — the spring is a small druidic site), bring it to Edril, and let him decide what to do with it. The wrong answer is to keep the coin or to interfere with the spring's water flow; either disrupts the small balance Edril has spent decades maintaining.

Reward: Edril teaches Kitty a single Aerdy-coastal small-rite for asking the sea to return a thing it has carried away. (One-shot, GM's discretion. Useful at most once per phase.)

4. Gianni — "The Track That Was Not Common" (ranger affinity)

Lieutenant Mada has noted in the coastal-road incident log a track three weeks old, two miles north of the city, that none of his garrison patrol can identify. He would like Gianni's eye on the tracks before he commits a militia detail.

The track is from a griffon (CR 2 SRD entry) — a young one, recently fledged, that drifted south from a coastal nesting site and has been picking off small livestock from a herder's flock without yet hunting humans. The griffon is not aggressive but is hungry and inexperienced.

How it resolves: Gianni rides north on the coastal road with Lieutenant Mada and one of his junior archers. Survival DC 13 to identify the track from the herder's confused descriptions. The right answer is to track the griffon back to its temporary nest (a cliff alcove half a mile inland), confirm it is young and not yet a threat to humans, and brief Mada that a humane removal is possible — the griffon can be captured and returned to a coastal nesting site with the help of a senior Aerdy ranger Mada knows in the southern districts. The wrong answer is to engage the griffon directly; it is a young CR 2 creature but Gianni is not equipped for it solo.

Reward: Lieutenant Mada formally extends Gianni's standing at the imperial garrison. She gains free use of the imperial training yard for the rest of the campaign and a written authorization to enter any Aerdy imperial customs office on the coastal road.

5. Selvara — "The Pre-Suel Folio" (sorcerer affinity)

Magister Vaden has acquired a small folio of pre-Suel sorcery via an estate auction and does not have time to catalogue it. He would like Selvara, during her visit, to read three of the folio's six volumes and produce a brief written summary.

The folio is genuine and old (approximately 1100 CY, a thousand years before the campaign). Three of the six volumes are routine pre-Suel theory: inheritance of sorcerous blood; the management of unwilled manifestations in childhood; the folk-medical traditions for sorcerers' migraines (which were a documented phenomenon then and are quietly so now). The other three volumes are more idiosyncratic and outside Selvara's brief; Magister Vaden will examine them himself when he has time.

How it resolves: Selvara reads the three assigned volumes over two to three days at the College's quiet annex. Investigation DC 12 to read pre-Suel paleography accurately (Selvara has not encountered it before, but Vaden has trained her enough during her visits that the DC is achievable). The right answer is a clear, modest summary that does not overclaim what the volumes contain — Vaden values accuracy over brilliance. The wrong answer is to invent connections to Selvara's own sorcerous experience that the texts do not support; Vaden will gently correct.

Reward: Magister Vaden grants Selvara permanent access to the College's restricted-arcane reading list. She may borrow up to two folio volumes at a time, with appropriate Aerdy-formal book-care discipline. Master Ferrick will be quietly proud when he hears.
